portal.name: usefulapi
portal.tagline: Hosted MCP servers for the tools you already use.

servers.name: Linear
servers.description: Search, read and create Linear issues, projects, teams and cycles.

tools.name: linear_search_issues
tools.title: Search / filter issues
tools.description: Find issues, optionally filtered by a text query (matches title), team key, workflow-state name, or assignee email. Read-only. GraphQL: query issues(filter, first).

tools.name: linear_get_issue
tools.title: Get one issue
tools.description: Fetch a single issue by its UUID or human identifier (e.g. \

tools.name: linear_list_teams
tools.title: List teams
tools.description: List the teams in the workspace (id, key, name). Use a team's id for linear_create_issue or its key for linear_search_issues. Read-only. GraphQL: query teams.

tools.name: linear_list_projects
tools.title: List projects
tools.description: List projects in the workspace (id, name, state, progress, target date). Read-only. GraphQL: query projects.

tools.name: linear_my_issues
tools.title: My assigned issues
tools.description: List issues assigned to the current user (the API key's owner / OAuth actor) via viewer.assignedIssues. Read-only. GraphQL: query viewer { assignedIssues }.

tools.name: linear_list_cycles
tools.title: List cycles
tools.description: List cycles (sprints), optionally scoped to a team by key, with number, name and start/end dates. Read-only. GraphQL: query cycles(filter).

tools.name: linear_create_issue
tools.title: Create issue (WRITE — mutates Linear)
tools.description: ⚠️ WRITE / MUTATING: creates a new issue in Linear. Requires a teamId (from linear_list_teams) and a title. Optional description (markdown), priority (0=none,1=urgent,2=high,3=medium,4=low), and assigneeId. GraphQL: mutation issueCreate.

tools.name: linear_add_comment
tools.title: Add comment (WRITE — mutates Linear)
tools.description: ⚠️ WRITE / MUTATING: posts a comment on an issue. Requires the issueId (UUID) and a markdown body. GraphQL: mutation commentCreate.

tools.name: linear_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: linear_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Linear tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: linear_upgrade_team
tools.title: Upgrade workspace to Team (unlimited for everyone)
tools.description: Subscribe your entire Linear workspace to the Team plan (unlimited seats) for UNLIMITED Linear tool calls for EVERY member of the workspace (the free tier caps monthly usage per user). Choose monthly ($99/month) or yearly billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment the whole workspace upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

servers.name: Better Stack
servers.description: Read monitors, incidents, heartbeats, on-call and status pages; acknowledge or resolve incidents.

tools.name: list_monitors
tools.title: List monitors
tools.description: List uptime monitors with their status (up/down/paused/pending/maintenance/validating), check frequency, and alert settings. Good first call to discover what is being monitored. Uptime API: GET /api/v2/monitors.

tools.name: get_monitor
tools.title: Get a monitor
tools.description: Get a single monitor by id, including current status, last_checked_at, SSL/HTTP settings, regions, and alerting configuration. Uptime API: GET /api/v2/monitors/{id}.

tools.name: list_incidents
tools.title: List incidents
tools.description: List incidents (alerts triggered by monitors/heartbeats), filterable by date range, monitor, and acknowledged/resolved state. Each incident carries cause, started_at, acknowledged_at, resolved_at. Uptime API: GET /api/v3/incidents.

tools.name: get_incident
tools.title: Get an incident
tools.description: Get a single incident by id, including cause, status, timeline timestamps (started/acknowledged/resolved), who acknowledged/resolved it, and the related monitor. Uptime API: GET /api/v3/incidents/{id}.

tools.name: list_heartbeats
tools.title: List heartbeats
tools.description: List heartbeats (cron/background-job monitors that expect a periodic ping), with period, grace, and status (up/down/paused/pending). Uptime API: GET /api/v2/heartbeats.

tools.name: list_on_call
tools.title: List on-call schedules
tools.description: List on-call calendars/schedules and the users currently on call for escalation. Useful to see who gets paged. Uptime API: GET /api/v2/on-calls.

tools.name: list_status_pages
tools.title: List status pages
tools.description: List public/private status pages with company name, subdomain, aggregate_state, and published flag. Uptime API: GET /api/v2/status-pages.

tools.name: acknowledge_incident
tools.title: Acknowledge an incident
tools.description: Acknowledge an ongoing incident, signalling that someone is looking into it (stops further escalation). Idempotent-ish: acknowledging an already-acknowledged incident returns HTTP 409. Uptime API: POST /api/v3/incidents/{id}/acknowledge.

tools.name: resolve_incident
tools.title: Resolve an incident
tools.description: Resolve an ongoing incident, marking it as handled/closed. Idempotent-ish: resolving an already-resolved incident returns HTTP 409. Uptime API: POST /api/v3/incidents/{id}/resolve.

tools.name: betterstack_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: betterstack_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Better Stack tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

servers.name: Loops
servers.description: Manage Loops contacts, mailing lists, and transactional/event emails.

tools.name: loops_test_api_key
tools.title: Test API key
tools.description: Verify the configured Loops API key and return the team name — a good first call. Loops API: GET /v1/api-key.

tools.name: loops_find_contact
tools.title: Find a contact
tools.description: Find a contact by email or userId (supply at least one). Loops API: GET /v1/contacts/find.

tools.name: loops_list_contact_properties
tools.title: List contact properties
tools.description: List contact fields / custom properties defined in Loops. Loops API: GET /v1/contacts/properties.

tools.name: loops_list_mailing_lists
tools.title: List mailing lists
tools.description: List all mailing lists in the Loops account (id + name). Loops API: GET /v1/lists.

tools.name: loops_create_contact
tools.title: Create a contact
tools.description: Creates a contact in Loops (additive). Loops API: POST /v1/contacts/create.

tools.name: loops_update_contact
tools.title: Update a contact
tools.description: Updates (or creates) a contact by email/userId. Upserts — creates the contact if not found. Loops API: PUT /v1/contacts/update.

tools.name: loops_delete_contact
tools.title: Delete a contact
tools.description: Deletes a contact from Loops. Destructive. Supply email OR userId. Loops API: POST /v1/contacts/delete.

tools.name: loops_create_contact_property
tools.title: Create a contact property
tools.description: Creates a custom contact property/field (additive schema change). Loops API: POST /v1/contacts/properties.

tools.name: loops_send_event
tools.title: Send an event
tools.description: Sends an event to Loops for a contact — TRIGGERS any automations/loops subscribed to this event. Loops API: POST /v1/events/send.

tools.name: loops_send_transactional
tools.title: Send a transactional email
tools.description: SENDS a real transactional email via a Loops template. This dispatches an actual email to the recipient. Loops API: POST /v1/transactional.

tools.name: loops_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: loops_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Loops tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

servers.name: Mixpanel
servers.description: Query Mixpanel events, funnels, retention, segmentation and insights (JQL).

tools.name: mixpanel_top_events
tools.title: Top events (today)
tools.description: List today's top events with counts and % change vs yesterday. Good first call to discover what events a project tracks. Query API: GET /events/top.

tools.name: mixpanel_segmentation
tools.title: Segment an event
tools.description: Count/segment/filter a single event over a date range. e.g. 'Signed up' by country last week. Params `on`/`where` use Mixpanel segmentation expressions like properties[\

tools.name: mixpanel_retention
tools.title: Retention report
tools.description: Cohort retention: of users who did `born_event`, how many came back and did `event` over subsequent intervals. Query API: GET /retention.

tools.name: mixpanel_list_funnels
tools.title: List saved funnels
tools.description: Get the funnel_id and name of every saved funnel in the project. Query API: GET /funnels/list.

tools.name: mixpanel_query_funnel
tools.title: Query a saved funnel
tools.description: Get conversion data for a saved funnel (use mixpanel_list_funnels to find funnel_id). Query API: GET /funnels.

tools.name: mixpanel_query_insights
tools.title: Query a saved Insights report
tools.description: Fetch the computed data behind a saved Insights report by bookmark_id (the report id in the board URL). Mixpanel's recommended, non-deprecated path for programmatic reporting. Query API: GET /insights.

tools.name: mixpanel_jql
tools.title: Run a JQL query
tools.description: Run a custom JQL (JavaScript Query Language) script for arbitrary analysis. Power-user escape hatch. Script must define main(). Query API: POST /jql. (Upstream maintenance mode; 2-min timeout, 5GB/query limit.)

tools.name: mixpanel_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: mixpanel_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Mixpanel tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

servers.name: Fireworks AI
servers.description: Inspect Fireworks AI models, deployments, datasets and fine-tuning jobs.

tools.title: Get account
tools.description: Get the Fireworks account record (display name, state, etc.). Control-plane: GET /v1/accounts/{account_id}.

tools.name: fireworks_list_models

tools.name: fireworks_get_model
tools.title: Get model

tools.name: fireworks_list_deployments
tools.title: List deployments
tools.description: List deployments (serving instances) in the account. Control-plane: GET /v1/accounts/{account_id}/deployments.

tools.name: fireworks_get_deployment
tools.title: Get deployment
tools.description: Get a single deployment by id. Control-plane: GET /v1/accounts/{account_id}/deployments/{deployment_id}.

tools.name: fireworks_list_deployed_models
tools.title: List deployed models
tools.description: List deployed models (model↔deployment bindings). Control-plane: GET /v1/accounts/{account_id}/deployedModels.

tools.name: fireworks_list_datasets
tools.title: List datasets
tools.description: List datasets in the account. Control-plane: GET /v1/accounts/{account_id}/datasets.

tools.name: fireworks_get_dataset

tools.description: Get a single dataset by id. Control-plane: GET /v1/accounts/{account_id}/datasets/{dataset_id}.

tools.name: fireworks_list_fine_tuning_jobs

tools.description: List supervised fine-tuning jobs in the account. Control-plane: GET /v1/accounts/{account_id}/supervisedFineTuningJobs.

tools.name: fireworks_get_fine_tuning_job
tools.title: Get fine-tuning job
tools.description: Get a single supervised fine-tuning job by id. Control-plane: GET /v1/accounts/{account_id}/supervisedFineTuningJobs/{job_id}.

tools.name: fireworks_list_batch_inference_jobs
tools.title: List batch inference jobs
tools.description: List batch inference jobs in the account. Control-plane: GET /v1/accounts/{account_id}/batchInferenceJobs.

tools.name: fireworks_list_users
tools.title: List users
tools.description: List account members (users). Control-plane: GET /v1/accounts/{account_id}/users.

tools.name: fireworks_create_dataset
tools.title: Create dataset
tools.description: Creates a dataset metadata record in Fireworks (additive; actual data is uploaded separately). Control-plane: POST /v1/accounts/{account_id}/datasets.

tools.name: fireworks_delete_deployment
tools.title: Delete deployment
tools.description: DELETES (shuts down) a Fireworks deployment — stops serving that model and stops its billing. Destructive. Control-plane: DELETE /v1/accounts/{account_id}/deployments/{deployment_id}.

tools.name: fireworks_usage_status

servers.name: Uploadcare
servers.description: Manage Uploadcare files, groups and webhooks; upload and store files.

tools.name: uploadcare_get_project
tools.title: Get project
tools.description: Get project details: name, public key, collaborators, and whether autostore is enabled. REST API: GET /project/.

tools.name: uploadcare_list_files
tools.title: List files
tools.description: List files in the project (paginated envelope: next, previous, total, per_page, results). REST API: GET /files/.

tools.name: uploadcare_get_file
tools.title: Get file
tools.description: Get full info for a single file: uuid, url, original_filename, size, mime_type, is_image, is_ready, timestamps, variations, content_info, metadata, tags. REST API: GET /files/{uuid}/.

tools.name: uploadcare_get_file_metadata
tools.title: Get file metadata
tools.description: Get all custom metadata key/value pairs for a file. REST API: GET /files/{uuid}/metadata/.

tools.name: uploadcare_list_groups
tools.title: List groups
tools.description: List file groups in the project (paginated envelope: next, previous, total, per_page, results). REST API: GET /groups/.

tools.name: uploadcare_get_group
tools.title: Get group
tools.description: Get info for a single file group by its id. REST API: GET /groups/{uuid}/.

tools.name: uploadcare_list_webhooks
tools.title: List webhooks
tools.description: List all webhook subscriptions configured for the project. REST API: GET /webhooks/.

tools.name: uploadcare_upload_from_url
tools.title: Upload file from URL
tools.description: Uploads a file into the project from a public URL (additive). Uses the Upload API host with the public key. Returns { type: \

tools.name: uploadcare_store_file
tools.title: Store file
tools.description: Marks a file as permanently stored (otherwise unstored files are deleted after 24h). Additive. REST API: PUT /files/{uuid}/storage/.

tools.name: uploadcare_delete_file
tools.title: Delete file
tools.description: Permanently deletes a file from the project. Destructive. REST API: DELETE /files/{uuid}/.

tools.name: uploadcare_create_webhook
tools.title: Create webhook
tools.description: Creates a webhook subscription (additive). The project will POST to target_url when the chosen event fires. REST API: POST /webhooks/.

tools.name: uploadcare_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)

servers.name: Cronitor
servers.description: Manage Cronitor monitors and send telemetry pings — list, inspect, create, update, delete.

tools.name: cronitor_list_monitors
tools.title: List monitors
tools.description: List monitors and their current passing/failing status. Cronitor API: GET /monitors (paginated via page + pageSize).

tools.name: cronitor_get_monitor
tools.title: Get monitor
tools.description: Get full detail for one monitor (latest_event, latest_invocations, passing status) — the 'is X healthy right now?' tool. Cronitor API: GET /monitors/{key}.

tools.name: cronitor_create_monitor
tools.title: Create monitor
tools.description: Creates a monitor in Cronitor (additive). Cronitor API: POST /monitors. Sends only the fields you provide.

tools.name: cronitor_update_monitor
tools.title: Update monitor
tools.description: Creates or updates a monitor by key (e.g. change schedule, pause via paused:true). Cronitor API: PUT /monitors with a single monitor object. Sends only the fields you provide.

tools.name: cronitor_delete_monitor
tools.title: Delete monitor
tools.description: PERMANENTLY deletes a monitor from Cronitor. Destructive and irreversible. Cronitor API: DELETE /monitors/{key}.

tools.name: cronitor_ping_monitor
tools.title: Ping monitor (telemetry)
tools.description: Reports a telemetry event (run/complete/fail/ok/pause) for a monitor — records job/heartbeat activity and can clear or trigger alerts. Cronitor telemetry: GET {telemetryUrl}/p/{apiKey}/{monitorKey}?state=...

tools.name: cronitor_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)

tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Cronitor tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

servers.name: MailerLite
servers.description: Read subscribers, groups, campaigns, fields, segments, automations, webhooks; safe additive writes.

tools.name: mailerlite_list_subscribers
tools.title: List subscribers
tools.description: List subscribers, optionally filtered by status. Cursor-paginated. MailerLite API: GET /subscribers.

tools.name: mailerlite_get_subscriber
tools.title: Get subscriber
tools.description: Fetch a single subscriber by id or email address. MailerLite API: GET /subscribers/{id}.

tools.name: mailerlite_list_groups
tools.title: List groups
tools.description: List subscriber groups/lists, optionally filtered by name. Page-paginated. MailerLite API: GET /groups.

tools.name: mailerlite_list_group_subscribers
tools.title: List group subscribers
tools.description: List the subscribers belonging to a group, optionally filtered by status. Cursor-paginated. MailerLite API: GET /groups/{group_id}/subscribers.

tools.name: mailerlite_list_campaigns
tools.title: List campaigns
tools.description: List campaigns, optionally filtered by status. Page-paginated. Read-only — this server does NOT send campaigns. MailerLite API: GET /campaigns.

tools.name: mailerlite_get_campaign

tools.description: Fetch a single campaign by id. Read-only. MailerLite API: GET /campaigns/{campaign_id}.

tools.name: mailerlite_list_fields
tools.title: List fields
tools.description: List custom/default subscriber fields, optionally filtered by type or keyword. Page-paginated. MailerLite API: GET /fields.

tools.name: mailerlite_list_segments
tools.title: List segments
tools.description: List segments. Page-paginated. MailerLite API: GET /segments.

tools.name: mailerlite_list_automations
tools.title: List automations
tools.description: List automations (workflows), optionally filtered by enabled state. Page-paginated. MailerLite API: GET /automations.

tools.name: mailerlite_list_webhooks
tools.title: List webhooks
tools.description: List configured webhooks. Page-paginated. MailerLite API: GET /webhooks.

tools.name: mailerlite_upsert_subscriber
tools.title: Upsert subscriber
tools.description: Creates or updates a subscriber (upsert by email). Additive — does NOT send any email. MailerLite API: POST /subscribers.

tools.name: mailerlite_create_group
tools.title: Create group
tools.description: Creates a subscriber group/list (additive). MailerLite API: POST /groups.

tools.name: mailerlite_assign_subscriber_to_group
tools.title: Assign subscriber to group
tools.description: Adds a subscriber to a group (additive membership). MailerLite API: POST /subscribers/{subscriber_id}/groups/{group_id}.

tools.name: mailerlite_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)

servers.name: Checkly
servers.description: Query Checkly synthetic monitoring — checks, statuses, results, alerts, reporting and dashboards.

tools.name: checkly_get_account
tools.title: Get current account
tools.description: Fetch details of the Checkly account tied to your credentials. Public API: GET /v1/accounts/me.

tools.name: checkly_list_checks
tools.title: List checks
tools.description: List all synthetic monitoring checks (API, browser, URL, heartbeat, etc.) in the account. Public API: GET /v1/checks.

tools.name: checkly_get_check
tools.title: Get a check
tools.description: Retrieve a single check's full configuration by id. Public API: GET /v1/checks/{id}.

tools.name: checkly_list_check_groups
tools.title: List check groups
tools.description: List all check groups (shared config for a set of checks). Public API: GET /v1/check-groups.

tools.name: checkly_get_check_group
tools.title: Get a check group
tools.description: Retrieve a single check group by id. Public API: GET /v1/check-groups/{id}.

tools.name: checkly_list_check_statuses
tools.title: List check statuses
tools.description: List the current pass/fail/degraded status of every check — the 'what's down right now' view. Public API: GET /v1/check-statuses.

tools.name: checkly_get_check_status
tools.title: Get check status
tools.description: Retrieve the current status details for a single check. Public API: GET /v1/check-statuses/{checkId}.

tools.name: checkly_list_check_results
tools.title: List check results
tools.description: List individual run results for a check (latency, success, location, response). Public API: GET /v1/check-results/{checkId}.

tools.name: checkly_get_check_result
tools.title: Get a check result
tools.description: Retrieve a single check-run result in full. Public API: GET /v1/check-results/{checkId}/{checkResultId}.

tools.name: checkly_list_check_alerts
tools.title: List check alerts
tools.description: List recent alert notifications fired across the whole account. Public API: GET /v1/check-alerts.

tools.name: checkly_get_check_alerts
tools.title: Get alerts for a check
tools.description: List alert notifications for one specific check. Public API: GET /v1/check-alerts/{checkId}.

tools.name: checkly_get_reporting
tools.title: Get reporting
tools.description: Aggregated availability / performance / SLA report across checks and groups. Public API: GET /v1/reporting.

tools.name: checkly_list_dashboards
tools.title: List dashboards
tools.description: List all public/private status dashboards. Public API: GET /v1/dashboards.

tools.name: checkly_list_alert_channels
tools.title: List alert channels
tools.description: List all alert channels (email, Slack, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) configured in the account. Public API: GET /v1/alert-channels.

tools.name: checkly_list_maintenance_windows
tools.title: List maintenance windows
tools.description: List all maintenance windows (periods where alerts are silenced). Public API: GET /v1/maintenance-windows.

tools.name: checkly_list_locations
tools.title: List locations
tools.description: List all public checking locations Checkly can run checks from. Public API: GET /v1/locations.

tools.name: checkly_list_private_locations
tools.title: List private locations
tools.description: List all private (self-hosted) checking locations. Public API: GET /v1/private-locations.

tools.name: checkly_list_snippets
tools.title: List snippets
tools.description: List all reusable code snippets. Public API: GET /v1/snippets.

tools.name: checkly_list_variables
tools.title: List variables
tools.description: List all account-level environment variables. Note: secret variable values are redacted by the API. Public API: GET /v1/variables.

tools.name: checkly_list_runtimes
tools.title: List runtimes
tools.description: List all supported runtimes (Node versions + bundled dependencies) for browser/API checks. Public API: GET /v1/runtimes.

tools.name: checkly_create_variable
tools.title: Create environment variable
tools.description: MODIFIES THE CHECKLY ACCOUNT: creates a new account-level environment variable. Additive (does not overwrite existing keys). Public API: POST /v1/variables.

tools.name: checkly_create_maintenance_window
tools.title: Create maintenance window
tools.description: MODIFIES THE CHECKLY ACCOUNT: schedules a maintenance window that silences alerts for a time range (e.g. during a deploy). Omit repeatUnit for a one-off window. Public API: POST /v1/maintenance-windows.

servers.name: Braintree
servers.description: Search Braintree transactions, customers and payment methods, and refund or void charges.

tools.name: braintree_find_transaction
tools.title: Find a transaction
tools.description: Fetch one transaction by its GraphQL global ID (the opaque `id` returned by braintree_search_transactions, NOT the short legacy id). Uses the GraphQL `node` query. Returns amount, status, timestamps, order id, and the linked customer.

tools.name: braintree_search_transactions
tools.title: Search transactions
tools.description: Search transactions by created-at date range, status, and/or customer. Builds a TransactionSearchInput (operators: status `in`, createdAt `greaterThanOrEqualTo`/`lessThanOrEqualTo`, customer.id `is`). Returns a page of transaction nodes (id, legacyId, amount, status, createdAt). GraphQL `search { transactions }`.

tools.name: braintree_find_customer
tools.title: Find a customer
tools.description: Fetch one customer by GraphQL global Customer ID via the `node` query. Returns id, legacyId, name, company, and created-at. To also enumerate stored cards/PayPal accounts use braintree_customer_payment_methods.

tools.name: braintree_customer_payment_methods
tools.title: List a customer's payment methods
tools.description: List the vaulted payment methods (credit cards, PayPal accounts, etc.) for one customer, by GraphQL global Customer ID. Returns each method's id, legacyId, usage, created-at, and detail type. GraphQL `node { ... on Customer { paymentMethods } }`.

tools.name: braintree_list_subscriptions
tools.title: List a customer's subscription activity
tools.description: List a customer's recurring / subscription payment activity. IMPORTANT: Braintree's GraphQL API does NOT expose native Subscription objects (recurring-billing management is still legacy-API only — see braintree/graphql-api issue #1). This tool therefore returns the customer's transactions (the observable evidence of subscription charges) via a TransactionSearchInput scoped to the customer; inspect each node's status/amount/createdAt to trace recurring charges. For full subscription CRUD you must use Braintree's legacy server SDK / REST API.

tools.name: braintree_refund_transaction
tools.title: Refund a transaction
tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE. Refund a SETTLED (or settling) transaction, returning money to the customer. Use the GraphQL global transaction ID. Omit `amount` for a full refund, or pass a decimal string for a partial refund. GraphQL mutation `refundTransaction`. For transactions not yet settled, use braintree_void_transaction instead.

tools.name: braintree_void_transaction

tools.description: DESTRUCTIVE. Void (cancel) a transaction that has NOT yet settled — e.g. an authorization or one submitted-for-settlement. Use the GraphQL global transaction ID. GraphQL mutation `voidTransaction`. For already-settled transactions use braintree_refund_transaction instead.

tools.name: amberflo_list_customers
tools.title: List customers
tools.description: List every customer registered in your Amberflo account with their ids, names, emails, traits and enabled status — the usual first call to discover customer ids.

tools.name: amberflo_get_customer
tools.title: Get customer
tools.description: Fetch a single customer by its Amberflo customerId, returning that customer's full profile and traits.

tools.name: amberflo_list_meters
tools.title: List meters
tools.description: List all meter definitions in your account (id, meterApiName, label, type and aggregation) so you know which meters the usage tools can target.

tools.name: amberflo_get_usage
tools.title: Get usage
tools.description: Query aggregated usage for a single meter over a time range, optionally grouped by dimension, filtered, and limited to the top N groups.

tools.name: amberflo_get_all_usage
tools.title: Get all usage
tools.description: Get total usage across all meters over a time range, optionally grouped by customer or restricted to a single customer.

tools.name: amberflo_explain_usage
tools.title: Explain usage
tools.description: Drill into the raw meter events behind a meter's usage over a time range to debug why an aggregated number looks off.

tools.name: amberflo_get_customer_plan
tools.title: Get customer plan
tools.description: Get a customer's latest assigned pricing product plan so you can see exactly what they are currently billed on.

tools.name: amberflo_list_customer_plan_history
tools.title: List customer plan history
tools.description: List the full history of pricing product plans assigned to a customer over time.

tools.name: amberflo_list_customer_invoices
tools.title: List customer invoices
tools.description: List all invoices for a customer, optionally scoped to one product, each with its payment status.

tools.name: amberflo_get_customer_invoice
tools.title: Get customer invoice
tools.description: Get a single customer invoice — either the current open invoice or a specific past one addressed by plan, year, month and day.

tools.name: amberflo_list_prepaid_orders
tools.title: List prepaid orders
tools.description: List a customer's active prepaid credit orders for a given product.

tools.name: amberflo_create_customer
tools.title: Create customer
tools.description: Register a new customer in Amberflo (id and name required, plus optional email, enabled flag and traits) and optionally auto-create it in Stripe.

tools.name: amberflo_assign_customer_plan
tools.title: Assign customer plan
tools.description: Attach a pricing product plan to a customer, changing what that customer is billed on going forward.

tools.name: amberflo_ingest_usage
tools.title: Ingest usage
tools.description: Record a single usage meter event to Amberflo's ingestion host, writing billable usage data for a customer.

tools.name: amberflo_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: amberflo_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Amberflo tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

servers.name: Anvil
servers.description: Hosted MCP server for Anvil — fill & generate PDFs and run Etch e-signature packets for AI agents.

tools.name: anvil_current_user
tools.title: Current user
tools.description: Get the authenticated Anvil user behind the API key plus every organization they belong to (eid, name, slug) — the best first call to discover the org identifiers other tools need.

tools.name: anvil_get_organization
tools.title: Get organization
tools.description: Fetch an organization by slug or eid together with its PDF templates (casts) and workflows (welds), so you can browse what is available to fill or submit.

tools.name: anvil_get_cast
tools.title: Get cast
tools.description: Fetch a cast — an Anvil PDF template — by eid, returning its title, version number, and template flag; its eid is what you pass to anvil_fill_pdf.

tools.name: anvil_get_etch_packet
tools.title: Get etch packet
tools.description: Fetch an Etch e-signature packet by eid, including its status, details URL, document group, and every signer's name, email, routing order, and signing status — the way to check signature progress.

tools.name: anvil_get_weld
tools.title: Get weld
tools.description: Fetch a weld — an Anvil workflow — by eid along with its forges, the individual form steps (eid, name, slug).

tools.name: anvil_get_weld_data
tools.title: Get weld data
tools.description: Fetch a single weldData submission to a weld by eid, reporting its completion state, test flag, and creation time.

tools.name: anvil_fill_pdf
tools.title: Fill pdf
tools.description: Fill an Anvil PDF template (cast) with a map of field aliases to values and return the finished PDF as base64, ready to download or forward.

tools.name: anvil_generate_pdf
tools.title: Generate pdf
tools.description: Generate a brand-new PDF from raw HTML or Markdown and return it as base64, with optional CSS styling and embedded title metadata.

tools.name: anvil_create_etch_packet
tools.title: Create etch packet
tools.description: Create an Etch e-signature packet from documents and signers; it defaults to a safe draft that sends nothing, and only emails signers when you set isDraft=false.

tools.name: anvil_generate_etch_sign_url
tools.title: Generate etch sign url
tools.description: Generate a one-time embedded signing URL for a specific signer in an Etch packet, for iframe or redirect signing flows.

tools.name: anvil_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: anvil_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Anvil tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

servers.name: Api2Pdf
servers.description: MCP server for Api2Pdf — generate PDFs & images from HTML, URLs or office files; merge, barcodes.

tools.name: api2pdf_get_balance
tools.title: Get balance
tools.description: Return the remaining credit balance and usage for your Api2Pdf account without generating anything billable.

tools.name: api2pdf_get_status
tools.title: Get status
tools.description: Report the current Api2Pdf service health so you can confirm the API is operational before generating files.

tools.name: api2pdf_html_to_pdf
tools.title: Html to pdf
tools.description: Render an HTML string to a PDF with headless Chrome and return a short-lived download link (or base64).

tools.name: api2pdf_url_to_pdf
tools.title: Url to pdf
tools.description: Fetch a live web page by URL and render it to a PDF with headless Chrome, returning a hosted link.

tools.name: api2pdf_markdown_to_pdf
tools.title: Markdown to pdf
tools.description: Render a Markdown string to a polished PDF via headless Chrome and return a hosted download link.

tools.name: api2pdf_html_to_image
tools.title: Html to image
tools.description: Render an HTML string to a PNG image with headless Chrome, returning a link or base64 bytes.

tools.name: api2pdf_url_to_image
tools.title: Url to image
tools.description: Capture a full-page screenshot of a live web page by URL using headless Chrome.

tools.name: api2pdf_wkhtml_to_pdf
tools.title: Wkhtml to pdf
tools.description: Render HTML to a PDF using the wkhtmltopdf engine, with optional table-of-contents support.

tools.name: api2pdf_office_to_pdf
tools.title: Office to pdf
tools.description: Convert an office document (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, ODT and more) at a URL to a PDF via LibreOffice.

tools.name: api2pdf_merge_pdfs
tools.title: Merge pdfs
tools.description: Merge several existing PDFs (given as URLs, in order) into a single combined PDF.

tools.name: api2pdf_compress_pdf
tools.title: Compress pdf
tools.description: Compress an existing PDF given by URL to reduce its file size, returning a hosted download link.

tools.name: api2pdf_extract_pages
tools.title: Extract pages
tools.description: Extract a range of pages (0-based indices) from an existing PDF by URL into a new PDF.

tools.name: api2pdf_add_password
tools.title: Add password
tools.description: Encrypt an existing PDF with a user password to open and an optional owner password for permissions.

tools.name: api2pdf_convert_to_markdown
tools.title: Convert to markdown
tools.description: Convert a document or file at a URL (PDF, Office and more) to Markdown text via MarkItDown for agents.

tools.name: api2pdf_generate_barcode
tools.title: Generate barcode
tools.description: Generate a barcode or QR code image (QRCode, Code128, PDF417, DataMatrix and more) from a value.

tools.name: api2pdf_delete_file
tools.title: Delete file
tools.description: Delete a previously generated file by its ResponseId to clean up hosted output.

tools.name: api2pdf_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: api2pdf_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Api2Pdf tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

servers.name: api.video
servers.description: Manage videos and live streams, inspect captions, players and webhooks, and read play analytics.

tools.name: apivideo_list_videos
tools.title: List videos
tools.description: List the videos in your account with optional filters by title, tags, metadata, description, or source live-stream id, plus sorting and pagination.

tools.name: apivideo_get_video
tools.title: Get video
tools.description: Fetch a single video's full object by id, including its title, encoded assets, player URL, and status flags.

tools.name: apivideo_get_video_status
tools.title: Get video status
tools.description: Check a video's ingest and encoding status, including upload progress, the encoded qualities available, and whether it is playable yet.

tools.name: apivideo_list_live_streams
tools.title: List live streams
tools.description: List the live streams in your account with optional filters by exact stream key or name and sorting.

tools.name: apivideo_get_live_stream
tools.title: Get live stream
tools.description: Retrieve a single live stream by id, including its stream key, RTMP ingest URL, and player URL for broadcasting.

tools.name: apivideo_list_captions
tools.title: List captions
tools.description: List the caption tracks attached to a video, showing each track's language, source URL, and default flag.

tools.name: apivideo_list_chapters
tools.title: List chapters
tools.description: List the chapter tracks defined for a video so an agent can inspect its timeline markers.

tools.name: apivideo_list_players
tools.title: List players
tools.description: List the customized player themes configured in the account that can be attached to videos or live streams.

tools.name: apivideo_list_webhooks
tools.title: List webhooks
tools.description: List the webhooks configured in the account, optionally filtered by a specific event name.

tools.name: apivideo_get_video_analytics
tools.title: Get video analytics
tools.description: Read aggregated video play analytics over a date range, grouped by a dimension such as videoId, country, device type, operating system, or browser.

tools.name: apivideo_get_live_stream_analytics
tools.title: Get live stream analytics
tools.description: Read aggregated live-stream play analytics over a date range, grouped by a chosen dimension such as liveStreamId, country, or device type.

tools.name: apivideo_create_video
tools.title: Create video
tools.description: Create a new video object, optionally ingesting from a public source URL, with title, description, tags, metadata, privacy, and player settings.

tools.name: apivideo_update_video
tools.title: Update video
tools.description: Update fields on an existing video such as its title, description, tags, metadata, privacy flag, or attached player theme.

tools.name: apivideo_delete_video
tools.title: Delete video
tools.description: Permanently delete a video and all of its assets — this action cannot be undone.

tools.name: apivideo_create_live_stream
tools.title: Create live stream
tools.description: Create a new live stream and get back a stream key and RTMP ingest URL to broadcast to.

tools.name: apivideo_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: apivideo_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED api.video tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

servers.name: Autumn
servers.description: MCP server for Autumn — read customers, plans, balances & invoices; track usage and attach plans.

tools.name: autumn_get_customer
tools.title: Get customer
tools.description: Fetch a single Autumn customer by id, optionally expanding related data such as invoices, entities, subscriptions and feature balances.

tools.name: autumn_list_customers
tools.title: List customers
tools.description: List and filter customers with cursor pagination, narrowing by plan, subscription status, processor or a free-text search over id, name and email.

tools.name: autumn_check
tools.title: Check
tools.description: Run a read-only entitlement check to see whether a customer has access to (or enough balance of) a feature, optionally including paywall/upsell preview info.

tools.name: autumn_list_invoices
tools.title: List invoices
tools.description: List invoices with cursor pagination, filtering by customer, entity, invoice status (draft/open/paid/void) or payment processor.

tools.name: autumn_get_entity
tools.title: Get entity
tools.description: Fetch a single entity (for example a seat) belonging to a customer, optionally expanding its related billing data.

tools.name: autumn_list_entities
tools.title: List entities
tools.description: List a customer's entities with cursor pagination and filters by plan, subscription status, processor or a search over id and name.

tools.name: autumn_list_features
tools.title: List features
tools.description: List every feature defined in the current Autumn environment so you know which features the balance and usage tools can target.

tools.name: autumn_get_feature
tools.title: Get feature
tools.description: Fetch a single feature by its id, returning that feature's full definition.

tools.name: autumn_list_plans
tools.title: List plans
tools.description: List all plans (products) in the environment, optionally including per-customer eligibility, archived plans or every plan version.

tools.name: autumn_get_plan
tools.title: Get plan
tools.description: Fetch a single plan by id, optionally pinned to a specific version instead of the latest.

tools.name: autumn_preview_attach
tools.title: Preview attach
tools.description: Preview the cost and effect of attaching a plan to a customer without charging or changing anything — a safe dry run before autumn_attach_plan.

tools.name: autumn_request
tools.title: Request
tools.description: Power-user read escape hatch that POSTs to any Autumn .get/.list/.check/.preview_* endpoint not covered by a dedicated tool, with write paths rejected before any request is sent.

tools.name: autumn_get_or_create_customer
tools.title: Get or create customer
tools.description: Fetch a customer by id, creating them (optionally in Stripe too) if they do not yet exist in Autumn.

tools.name: autumn_update_customer
tools.title: Update customer
tools.description: Partially update a customer's name, email, fingerprint, metadata, Stripe id, currency or id — only the fields you provide change.

tools.name: autumn_track_usage
tools.title: Track usage
tools.description: Record a usage event that deducts or credits a customer's feature balance, addressed by either feature_id or event_name.

tools.name: autumn_attach_plan
tools.title: Attach plan
tools.description: Attach (subscribe) a plan to a customer, which may create an invoice or return a Stripe payment URL for checkout.

tools.name: autumn_create_entity
tools.title: Create entity
tools.description: Create an entity (for example a seat) under a customer and tie it to a feature, with optional per-entity billing controls.

tools.name: autumn_open_customer_portal
tools.title: Open customer portal
tools.description: Create a Stripe billing-portal session so a customer can manage their own subscription, returning the portal URL.

tools.name: autumn_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: autumn_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Autumn tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

servers.name: Bannerbear
servers.description: Generate images, videos, and screenshots from your Bannerbear templates via AI.

tools.name: bannerbear_get_account
tools.title: Get account
tools.description: Fetch your Bannerbear account details and usage — current API usage, quota, and paid plan name — so an agent can check remaining credits before firing renders.

tools.name: bannerbear_list_templates
tools.title: List templates
tools.description: List the image templates in your project, filterable by name, tag, and page, so an agent can discover which designs are available to render.

tools.name: bannerbear_get_template
tools.title: Get template
tools.description: Fetch a single template by uid including its available_modifications — the exact layers you can override — so an agent knows what it can change before generating.

tools.name: bannerbear_list_template_sets
tools.title: List template sets
tools.description: List the template sets in your project, the groupings used to render whole collections of images in one call.

tools.name: bannerbear_get_template_set
tools.title: Get template set
tools.description: Fetch a single template set by uid along with the templates it contains, so an agent can plan a multi-image collection render.

tools.name: bannerbear_list_images
tools.title: List images
tools.description: List the generated images in your project with pagination, giving an agent a history of past renders and their media URLs.

tools.name: bannerbear_get_image
tools.title: Get image
tools.description: Fetch a single generated image by uid — poll this to watch an async render move from pending to completed and pick up its final image_url.

tools.name: bannerbear_get_video
tools.title: Get video
tools.description: Fetch a single generated video by uid — poll this to watch an async video render complete and retrieve its video_url.

tools.name: bannerbear_get_collection
tools.title: Get collection
tools.description: Fetch a single collection by uid — poll this to watch a template-set render complete and collect the map of per-template image URLs.

tools.name: bannerbear_list_fonts
tools.title: List fonts
tools.description: List every font available for use in template text modifications, so an agent can pick a valid font before overriding a text layer.

tools.name: bannerbear_list_effects
tools.title: List effects
tools.description: List the image effects available for use in template modifications, so an agent can apply a supported effect when generating.

tools.name: bannerbear_create_image
tools.title: Create image
tools.description: Generate an image from a template with per-layer modifications; runs async by default (poll the returned uid) or set synchronous to block and receive the finished image_url in one call.

tools.name: bannerbear_create_collection
tools.title: Create collection
tools.description: Generate a whole collection of images from a template set in one call with shared modifications, then poll the returned uid for the finished per-template image URLs.

tools.name: bannerbear_create_screenshot
tools.title: Create screenshot
tools.description: Capture a screenshot of any public web page at a chosen viewport size (with optional mobile rendering); async by default or synchronous to wait for the finished capture.

tools.name: bannerbear_create_video
tools.title: Create video
tools.description: Generate a video from a video template, optionally driven by input media and per-layer modifications, then poll the returned uid for the finished video_url.

tools.name: bannerbear_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: bannerbear_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Bannerbear tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: cliniko_list_patients

tools.name: cliniko_get_patient

tools.name: cliniko_list_appointments
tools.title: List appointments

tools.name: cliniko_get_appointment
tools.title: Get appointment
tools.description: Fetch a single individual appointment by id with its full scheduling details.

tools.name: cliniko_list_practitioners
tools.title: List practitioners
tools.description: List the practitioners in the account.

tools.name: cliniko_list_businesses
tools.title: List businesses
tools.description: List the businesses (practice locations) in the account.

tools.name: cliniko_list_appointment_types
tools.title: List appointment types
tools.description: List the appointment types (service definitions) offered by the practice.

tools.name: cliniko_list_products
tools.title: List products
tools.description: List the billable products and items configured in the account.

tools.name: cliniko_list_invoices
tools.title: List invoices

tools.name: cliniko_get_invoice

tools.description: Fetch a single invoice by id with its line items and totals.

tools.name: cliniko_list_treatment_notes
tools.title: List treatment notes
tools.description: List treatment notes (protected health information), optionally filtered by patient — read-only, handle with care.

tools.name: cliniko_get_treatment_note
tools.title: Get treatment note
tools.description: Fetch a single treatment note by id (protected health information) — read-only.

tools.name: cliniko_list_available_times
tools.title: List available times
tools.description: List bookable appointment slots for a business, practitioner, and appointment type across a date range of up to seven days.

tools.name: cliniko_next_available_time
tools.title: Next available time
tools.description: Get the single next bookable slot for a business, practitioner, and appointment type, optionally from a given date.

tools.name: cliniko_create_patient
tools.title: Create patient
tools.description: Create a real new patient record in the live practice from a first and last name, plus optional contact and demographic details.

tools.name: cliniko_create_appointment
tools.title: Create appointment
tools.description: Book a real new individual appointment in the live practice for a patient, practitioner, business, and appointment type at a given start time.

tools.name: cliniko_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)

tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Cliniko tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. servers.name: Codat
servers.description: MCP server for Codat — companies, connections, invoices, bills and financial statements.

tools.name: codat_get_profile
tools.title: Get profile
tools.description: Fetch the authenticated Codat account's profile (organization name, redirect URLs, enabled features) as a quick way to confirm the API key works.

tools.name: codat_list_companies
tools.title: List companies
tools.description: List the companies in your Codat account — each representing one of your customers' businesses and holding its data connections — with query filters and pagination.

tools.name: codat_get_company
tools.title: Get company
tools.description: Fetch a single company by id, including its name, source platform, data connections, and created/last-sync timestamps.

tools.name: codat_list_connections
tools.title: List connections
tools.description: List a company's data connections, each linking it to one source platform such as QuickBooks or Xero, with their status and last sync.

tools.name: codat_get_connection
tools.title: Get connection
tools.description: Fetch a single data connection by id, including its platformKey, status, source type, and last sync time.

tools.name: codat_get_data_status
tools.title: Get data status
tools.description: Get the freshness and sync status of every accounting data type for a company so you know whether a dataset has been pulled before reading it.

tools.name: codat_get_data_info
tools.title: Get data info
tools.description: Get a company's accounting metadata, such as its base currency and the set of data types it supports.

tools.name: codat_list_integrations
tools.title: List integrations
tools.description: List the accounting, commerce, and banking platforms Codat supports, each with the platformKey used to create connections.

tools.name: codat_list_accounts
tools.title: List accounts
tools.description: List a company's chart of accounts with each account's name, nominal code, type, status, currency, and current balance.

tools.name: codat_list_invoices
tools.title: List invoices
tools.description: List a company's accounts-receivable invoices with numbers, customer refs, issue/due dates, currency, totals, amounts due, status, and line items.

tools.name: codat_get_invoice
tools.title: Get invoice
tools.description: Fetch a single accounts-receivable invoice by id in full detail, including line items and payment allocations.

tools.name: codat_list_bills
tools.title: List bills
tools.description: List a company's accounts-payable bills with supplier refs, issue/due dates, currency, totals, amounts due, status, and line items.

tools.name: codat_list_bill_payments
tools.title: List bill payments
tools.description: List a company's bill payments (money paid out to suppliers) with supplier refs, dates, currency, totals, and lines.

tools.name: codat_list_credit_notes
tools.title: List credit notes
tools.description: List a company's credit notes with numbers, customer refs, issue dates, currency, totals, remaining credit, and status.

tools.name: codat_list_customers
tools.title: List customers
tools.description: List a company's customers with names, contacts, email addresses, postal addresses, status, and default currency.

tools.name: codat_list_suppliers
tools.title: List suppliers
tools.description: List a company's suppliers/vendors with names, contacts, email addresses, addresses, status, and default currency.

tools.name: codat_list_payments
tools.title: List payments
tools.description: List a company's received payments (money in against invoices) with customer refs, dates, currency, totals, and allocations.

tools.name: codat_list_journal_entries
tools.title: List journal entries
tools.description: List a company's journal entries with posting dates and journal lines carrying account refs, net amounts, and currency.

tools.name: codat_get_balance_sheet
tools.title: Get balance sheet
tools.description: Get a company's balance sheet broken into periods, controlled by the required periodLength and periodsToCompare parameters.

tools.name: mercoa_get_entity
tools.title: Get entity
tools.description: Fetch a single entity's full record by its Mercoa entity ID or your own foreign ID.

tools.name: mercoa_get_entity_events
tools.title: Get entity events
tools.description: Retrieve the chronological event and audit log for an entity within an optional date range.

tools.name: mercoa_get_entity_invoice_metrics
tools.title: Get entity invoice metrics
tools.description: Aggregate an entity's payable and receivable invoice totals and counts, grouped by currency, status or date, for AP/AR dashboards and aging reports.

tools.name: mercoa_list_entity_payment_methods
tools.title: List entity payment methods
tools.description: List an entity's payment methods such as bank accounts, cards and checks.

tools.name: mercoa_get_payment_method
tools.title: Get payment method
tools.description: Fetch a single payment method belonging to an entity by its payment-method ID.

tools.name: mercoa_list_entity_users
tools.title: List entity users
tools.description: List the users attached to an entity, including approvers and admins.

tools.name: mercoa_find_invoices
tools.title: Find invoices
tools.description: Search and filter invoices (bills) across payers and vendors by status, entity, date, approver, payment type and more.

tools.name: mercoa_get_invoice
tools.title: Get invoice
tools.description: Fetch a single invoice (bill) with all of its details by invoice ID.

tools.name: mercoa_get_invoice_events
tools.title: Get invoice events
tools.description: Retrieve an invoice's event and audit log covering status changes, approvals and payments.

tools.name: mercoa_find_transactions
tools.title: Find transactions
tools.description: List and search payment transactions filtered by entity, invoice, status, type and date.

tools.name: mercoa_get_transaction
tools.title: Get transaction
tools.description: Fetch a single payment transaction by its transaction ID.

tools.name: mercoa_get_organization
tools.title: Get organization
tools.description: Get the caller's Mercoa organization configuration, including enabled payment methods and settings.

tools.name: mercoa_create_entity
tools.title: Create entity
tools.description: Create a new entity — customer, vendor, payor or payee — with role flags, account type and a nested business or individual profile.

tools.name: mercoa_create_invoice
tools.title: Create invoice
tools.description: Create a new invoice/bill with amount, currency, payer, vendor, dates and line items, staged as a draft or submitted.

tools.name: mercoa_update_invoice
tools.title: Update invoice
tools.description: Update an existing invoice/bill — advance its status or change amounts, dates, payment methods or line items.

tools.name: mercoa_add_invoice_comment
tools.title: Add invoice comment
tools.description: Add a comment to an invoice, recorded in its activity log.

tools.name: mercoa_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: mercoa_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Mercoa tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

servers.name: Northflank
servers.description: Inspect and control your Northflank projects, services, jobs, and builds from your AI assistant.

tools.name: northflank_list_projects
tools.title: List projects
tools.description: List the projects your API token can access (optionally scoped to a team) — call this first to discover the project ids that nearly every other tool requires.

tools.name: northflank_get_project
tools.title: Get project
tools.description: Get a single project's full details, including its region, cluster, and the services, jobs, and addons it contains.

tools.name: northflank_list_services
tools.title: List services
tools.description: List the services in a project — build, deployment, or combined — each with its current build and deployment status.

tools.name: northflank_get_service
tools.title: Get service
tools.description: Get a single service's configuration and current runtime status.

tools.name: northflank_list_jobs
tools.title: List jobs
tools.description: List the manual and cron jobs defined in a project.

tools.name: northflank_get_job
tools.title: Get job
tools.description: Get a single job's details, including its build/deploy configuration and cron schedule.

tools.name: northflank_list_addons
tools.title: List addons
tools.description: List the addons (managed databases and services) in a project along with each one's status.

tools.name: northflank_get_addon
tools.title: Get addon
tools.description: Get a single addon's details and current status.

tools.name: northflank_list_service_builds
tools.title: List service builds
tools.description: List a build service's build-run history, including branch, commit sha, status, and timestamps.

tools.name: northflank_get_service_metrics
tools.title: Get service metrics
tools.description: Get CPU and memory metrics for a service's containers, as a point-in-time snapshot or a time-series range.

tools.name: northflank_list_secret_groups
tools.title: List secret groups
tools.description: List a project's secret groups by metadata only (id, name, type, priority, restrictions) — never the secret values themselves.

tools.name: northflank_list_domains
tools.title: List domains
tools.description: List the custom domains registered on the account or a team, each with its verification status.

tools.name: northflank_trigger_service_build
tools.title: Trigger service build
tools.description: Start a new build for a build or combined service, optionally targeting a specific branch, commit sha, or pull request.

tools.name: northflank_restart_service
tools.title: Restart service
tools.description: Restart all of a service's running containers.

tools.name: northflank_scale_service
tools.title: Scale service
tools.description: Set the number of running instances (replicas) for a service.

tools.name: northflank_pause_service
tools.title: Pause service
tools.description: Pause a service, scaling it down to zero running instances.

tools.name: northflank_resume_service
tools.title: Resume service
tools.description: Resume a paused service, optionally restoring its instance count and CI/CD/autoscaling state.

tools.name: northflank_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: northflank_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Northflank tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

servers.name: Nylas
servers.description: MCP server for Nylas — read email, calendars, events and contacts, and send email or create events.

tools.name: nylas_list_grants
tools.title: List grants
tools.description: List the connected accounts (grants) on your Nylas application, each with the grant_id used to scope every other email, calendar, and contacts tool.

tools.name: nylas_get_grant
tools.title: Get grant
tools.description: Get the details of a single connected account (grant) by id, including its provider and connection state.

tools.name: nylas_list_messages
tools.title: List messages
tools.description: List email messages for a connected account, filterable by subject, sender, recipient, folder, and unread or starred status.

tools.name: nylas_get_message
tools.title: Get message
tools.description: Get a single email message by id, including its headers, body, and metadata.

tools.name: nylas_list_folders
tools.title: List folders
tools.description: List the mail folders and labels for a connected account.

tools.name: nylas_list_calendars
tools.title: List calendars
tools.description: List the calendars on a connected account, each with the calendar_id used by the events tools.

tools.name: nylas_get_calendar
tools.title: Get calendar
tools.description: Get a single calendar by id, including its name, timezone, and metadata.

tools.name: nylas_list_events
tools.title: List events
tools.description: List events on a given calendar over an optional start/end time window, optionally including cancelled events.

tools.name: nylas_get_event
tools.title: Get event
tools.description: Get a single calendar event by id from a given calendar, including its time, participants, and location.

tools.name: nylas_list_contacts
tools.title: List contacts
tools.description: List contacts for a connected account, filterable by email address or phone number.

tools.name: nylas_get_contact
tools.title: Get contact
tools.description: Get a single contact by id, including names, email addresses, phone numbers, and other details.

tools.name: nylas_list_drafts
tools.title: List drafts
tools.description: List the saved draft emails on a connected account.

tools.name: nylas_send_message
tools.title: Send message
tools.description: Send a real email from a connected account to the given recipients, with optional cc, bcc, and reply-to-message threading — destructive.

tools.name: nylas_create_event
tools.title: Create event
tools.description: Create a calendar event on a given calendar with a title, start/end time, description, and location, optionally inviting participants.

tools.name: nylas_create_draft
tools.title: Create draft
tools.description: Create (but do not send) a draft email on a connected account — safe and additive.

tools.name: nylas_usage_status
tools.title: Usage status (free-tier meter)
tools.description: Report the caller's current free-tier usage this month: calls used, monthly limit, remaining, and whether the cap is reached. Read-only; does not count against the meter.

tools.name: nylas_upgrade
tools.title: Upgrade to Pro (unlimited)
tools.description: Subscribe to the Pro plan for UNLIMITED Nylas tool calls (the free tier caps monthly usage). Choose monthly ($9/month) or yearly ($90/year — 2 months free) billing. Returns a Stripe Checkout link to open in your browser; after payment your account upgrades automatically. Read-only; does not count against the meter.
